Sister.Ship.Non.Compliance.Description: ADMIRAL KOLCHAK (9076208) was documented fishing 0.5nm inside the US EEZ by a USCG helicopter in 2014. The US issued the vessel owner a Notice of Violation and Assessment of civil penalty and sent the notice to the Russian Ministry of Justice for service of process on the vessel owner, but it appears there was no enforcement by Russia.  IKHTIOLOG (8804309) source indicates that a total of nine incursions over a period of four days were detected into the US EEZ while the vessel was fishing close to the Russia-US boundary in May/June 2022 (from wording it sounds like this was detected remotely, presumably over AIS). The Russian Border Guard investigated based on USCG report and fined the vessel owner for  failing to notify the Russian Federation of an MBL crossing. Source

ZHOU YU 929 refused to allow an at sea inspection by a US Coast Guard vessel in the SPRFMO convetion area close to the Galapagos islands in 2022. A motion was made to add this vessel and another China flagged vessel to the SPRFMO IUU list but this was blocked by China. FU YUAN YU 019 IATTC-authorized fishing vessel— retrieving longline gear in the IATTC Convention Area about 400 miles southwest of the Mexican coast. Sea Shepherd witnessed the vessel using metal branch lines running directly from longline floats in violation of a CMM (C-16-05) that prohibits the use of this type of gear, known as shark lines.

Sister.Ship.Non.Compliance.Description: FU YUAN YU 019 IATTC-authorized fishing vessel— retrieving longline gear in the IATTC Convention Area about 400 miles southwest of the Mexican coast. Sea Shepherd witnessed the vessel using metal branch lines running directly from longline floats in violation of a CMM (C-16-05) that prohibits the use of this type of gear, known as shark lines.

ZHOU YU 929 refused to allow an at sea inspection by a US Coast Guard vessel in the SPRFMO convetion area close to the Galapagos islands in 2022. A motion was made to add this vessel and another China flagged vessel to the SPRFMO IUU list but this was blocked by China. Multiple vessels in fleet have a history of AIS spoofing and transmission gaps. Reports of forced labor on the ZHOU YU 901 and 905.

Sister.Ship.Non.Compliance.Description: DONG CHENG 988 accused of illegal shark finning
Sources: C4ADS internal source available upon request

Vessel.Non.Compliance.Description: HORNG YIH FWU NO.628 was sanctioned by the Taiwan FA for holding prohibited fish species(OCS) and failing to dispose of shark fins in the prescribed manner. Taiwan FA fined the vessels operator (Bao-Yinn Homg) NTD 2,500,000 and the vessels captain NTD 500,000 as well as suspending their fishing license for 1 month.
JIN CHENG LI NO.1 was sanctioned by Taiwan Fisheries Agency for incorrect processing of shark fins in 2025, with the Operator fined 500,000 NTD
Sister.Ship.Non.Compliance.Description: A Greenpeace report identifies HE SHUN NO 6 (then called SHENG I HSING NO. 63) as one of a number of vessels that the NGO documented unloading shark fins not attached to carcasses in aTaiwanese ports during a three-month period in 2015, in contravention of Taiwan's shark finning regulation. The vessel has since changed ownership but at the time was listed as owned by Wang Chen
SisterShipWithNonComplianceConcerns: HE SHUN NO. 6 (ex-SHENG I HSING NO. 63)

Sister.Ship.Non.Compliance.Description: Vessel owner Wen Li Chen was listed at the time as the owner of CHIN SHUN KUO, one of a number of vessels indicated to have had encounters with with unauthorised mini-reefer NEW BAI I NO. 168 in the ICCAT area in 2016 (NEW BAI I NO. 168 was subsequently IUU listed by ICCAT)- based on document found onboard the vessel

IHS_TechnicalManager: K LINE RORO BULK SHIP MGMT CO
